title: cephaelis biternata
intro: Cephaelis biternata, also known as the bitter twin flower, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its bitter compounds. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Cephaelis biternata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves are also used in herbal remedies, such as tinctures and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties.
